John
Maus

The Music Messenger

Explore albumsSee text encoding
John MausDiscographyModelsText EncodingRDF

A signal in synth-pop.

John Maus is an American musician, composer, singer, songwriter and academic whose work is often associated with synth-pop, post-punk, hypnagogic pop, experimental pop and lo-fi aesthetics.

His music is recognizable for its dramatic baritone voice, cold keyboard textures, repeated slogans and a strange tension between pop directness and philosophical abstraction. The songs often feel simple at first, but they create a world of anxiety, romance, political language and theatrical distance.

For this project, John Maus is not treated only as a musician but as a cultural entity. The page becomes a starting point for identifying people, places, organizations, albums, concepts, citations and authority-control links. These elements are then modelled theoretically, formalized conceptually, encoded in TEI and transformed into RDF.

Discography as cultural data.

The albums are represented as works in the semantic model. Each release is connected to John Maus as creator, and some albums also connect to labels, concepts, dates and textual references.

2006 · John Maus album

Songs

Maus’s first official album introduces the raw version of his sound: distant vocals, lo-fi keyboards, simple melodic forms and a private theatrical feeling. In the project it is treated as an early musical work connected to John Maus as creator.

2007 · John Maus album

Love Is Real

This album deepens the haunted and romantic side of his music. It sounds devotional, synthetic and unstable, placing emotional pop gestures inside a strange minimal electronic atmosphere.

2011 · John Maus album

We Must Become the Pitiless Censors of Ourselves

The central album of this project. It connects the musical identity of John Maus with philosophical language, because the title is linked to Alain Badiou’s text Fifteen Theses on Contemporary Art. For this reason it appears in the TEI file as both a musical work and a textual/citation phenomenon.

2012 · John Maus album

A Collection of Rarities and Previously Unreleased Material

This compilation works like an archive of fragments: unreleased tracks, sketches and hidden materials around Maus’s musical development. It is useful for the project because it shows the discography as a documentary field, not only a list of commercial albums.

2017 · John Maus album

Screen Memories

Released after a long gap, Screen Memories presents a colder, more cinematic sound-world. Its imagery of screens, memory and apocalyptic atmosphere fits the project’s focus on mediation, representation and cultural memory.

2018 · John Maus album

Addendum

A companion-like release connected to the Screen Memories period. In the project it is represented as another music album created by John Maus and part of the semantic discography.

2025 · John Maus album

Later Than You Think

A later release that extends the timeline of the project. It keeps John Maus visible as an active cultural subject and shows how the RDF model can remain open to newer works.

Models.

The theoretical model explains the domain in natural language relations. The conceptual model translates the same relations into formal ontology terms.

Theoretical model

The theoretical model connects John Maus to albums, places, people, organizations, genres and authority-control information.

Conceptual model / ontology

The conceptual model uses classes and properties from existing vocabularies, including FOAF, Schema.org, SKOS, DCTerms, FaBiO and FRBR-style terms.

Text encoding.

This section now reflects the corrected workflow: a custom XML source document is transformed into a TEI scholarly encoding, then the TEI is used to generate both the HTML output and the RDF linked-data file.

The first file, john_maus_XML.xml, is a project-specific XML document. It stores the John Maus material in a simple structure: sections, paragraphs, people, places, organizations, albums, concepts and references.

The second file, john_maus_TEI.xml, rewrites that same material using TEI conventions. It uses elements such as persName, placeName, orgName, title, term, quote, listPerson, listOrg, listPlace, listBibl and listRelation.

01Custom XML source
02TEI scholarly encoding
03XSLT to HTML output
04Python extraction
05RDF/Turtle linked data

Custom XML source

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<artistBiography id="john-maus-project">
  <metadata>
    <title>John Maus</title>
    <subtitle>The Music Messenger</subtitle>
    <creator>Abderrahmane Afrit</creator>
    <course>Information Science and Cultural Heritage</course>
    <institution>University of Bologna</institution>
    <sources>
      <source id="source-wikipedia" type="wikipedia" url="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/John_Maus">John Maus</source>
      <source id="source-wikidata" type="wikidata" url="https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q5980315">John Maus Wikidata Entity</source>
      <source id="source-album" type="wikipedia" url="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/We_Must_Become_the_Pitiless_Censors_of_Ourselves">We Must Become the Pitiless Censors of Ourselves</source>
    </sources>
  </metadata>

  <text>
    <section id="identity" type="biography">
      <heading>Identity</heading>
      <paragraph>
        <person ref="johnMaus">John Maus</person> was born on <date when="1980-02-23">23 February 1980</date> in <place ref="austinMinnesota">Austin, Minnesota</place>. He is an American musician, singer, songwriter, composer and academic.
      </paragraph>
    </section>

    <section id="genres" type="music">
      <heading>Genres</heading>
      <paragraph>
        His music is associated with <concept ref="synthPop">synth-pop</concept>, <concept ref="hypnagogicPop">hypnagogic pop</concept>, <concept ref="postPunk">post-punk</concept>, <concept ref="experimentalPop">experimental pop</concept> and <concept ref="loFi">lo-fi</concept>. These genres are important because they connect the artist to underground pop, electronic music and experimental aesthetics.
      </paragraph>
    </section>

    <section id="education" type="education">
      <heading>Education</heading>
      <paragraph>
        <person ref="johnMaus">John Maus</person> studied at <organization ref="calArts">California Institute of the Arts</organization>, <organization ref="egs">European Graduate School</organization> and <organization ref="universityHawaii">University of Hawaiʻi</organization>. At <organization ref="calArts">California Institute of the Arts</organization>, he met <person ref="arielPink">Ariel Pink</person>, who became an important artistic connection.
      </paragraph>
    </section>

    <section id="related-people" type="network">
      <heading>Related People</heading>
      <paragraph>
        The project records <person ref="arielPink">Ariel Pink</person> as a collaborator, <person ref="pandaBear">Panda Bear</person> as a performance connection, and <person ref="alainBadiou">Alain Badiou</person> as a philosophical reference.
      </paragraph>
    </section>

    <section id="organizations" type="network">
      <heading>Organizations</heading>
      <paragraph>
        <person ref="johnMaus">John Maus</person> is connected to <organization ref="hauntedGraffiti">Ariel Pink's Haunted Graffiti</organization>, <organization ref="ribbonMusic">Ribbon Music</organization>, <organization ref="upsetTheRhythm">Upset the Rhythm</organization> and <organization ref="young">Young</organization>.
      </paragraph>
    </section>

    <section id="albums" type="discography">
      <heading>Albums</heading>
      <paragraph>
        Important albums include <work ref="songs">Songs</work>, <work ref="loveIsReal">Love Is Real</work>, <work ref="pitilessCensors">We Must Become the Pitiless Censors of Ourselves</work>, <work ref="rarities">A Collection of Rarities and Previously Unreleased Material</work>, <work ref="screenMemories">Screen Memories</work>, <work ref="addendum">Addendum</work> and <work ref="laterThanYouThink">Later Than You Think</work>.
      </paragraph>
    </section>

    <section id="textual-phenomenon" type="intertextuality">
      <heading>Textual Phenomenon</heading>
      <paragraph>
        The album title <quotation source="fifteenTheses">We Must Become the Pitiless Censors of Ourselves</quotation> is encoded as a textual reference because it recalls <work ref="fifteenTheses">Fifteen Theses on Contemporary Art</work> by <person ref="alainBadiou">Alain Badiou</person>.
      </paragraph>
    </section>
  </text>

  <entities>
    <people>
      <person id="johnMaus" wikidata="https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q5980315" wikipedia="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/John_Maus">John Maus</person>
      <person id="arielPink" wikidata="https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q47839" wikipedia="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ariel_Pink">Ariel Pink</person>

Corrected TEI encoding

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<TEI xmlns="http://www.tei-c.org/ns/1.0" xmlns:xml="http://www.w3.org/XML/1998/namespace">
  <teiHeader>
    <fileDesc>
      <titleStmt>
        <title>John Maus — TEI Text Encoding</title>
        <author>Abderrahmane Afrit</author>
        <respStmt>
          <resp>Text encoding and linked-data modelling</resp>
          <name>Abderrahmane Afrit</name>
        </respStmt>
      </titleStmt>
      <publicationStmt>
        <publisher>Information Science and Cultural Heritage project</publisher>
        <pubPlace>Bologna</pubPlace>
        <date when="2026-05-18">18 May 2026</date>
        <availability><p>Academic project file.</p></availability>
      </publicationStmt>
      <sourceDesc>
        <p>This TEI document was produced by transforming a custom XML biography of John Maus into TEI-compliant markup.</p>
        <listBibl>
          <bibl xml:id="source-wikipedia"><title>John Maus</title><author>Wikipedia contributors</author><ptr target="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/John_Maus"/></bibl>
          <bibl xml:id="source-wikidata"><title>John Maus Wikidata Entity</title><ptr target="https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q5980315"/></bibl>
          <bibl xml:id="source-album"><title>We Must Become the Pitiless Censors of Ourselves</title><ptr target="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/We_Must_Become_the_Pitiless_Censors_of_Ourselves"/></bibl>
        </listBibl>
      </sourceDesc>
    </fileDesc>
    <encodingDesc>
      <projectDesc>
        <p>The encoding identifies persons, places, organizations, works, concepts and intertextual references. Standoff lists are used so that the same entities can be reused for HTML transformation and RDF generation.</p>
      </projectDesc>
    </encodingDesc>
    <profileDesc><langUsage><language ident="en">English</language></langUsage></profileDesc>
  </teiHeader>
  <text>
    <body>
      <div type="biography" n="1">
        <head>Identity</head>
        <p><persName ref="#johnMaus">John Maus</persName> was born on <date when="1980-02-23">23 February 1980</date> in <placeName ref="#austinMinnesota">Austin, Minnesota</placeName>. He is an American <term ref="#musician">musician</term>, <term ref="#singer">singer</term>, <term ref="#songwriter">songwriter</term>, <term ref="#composer">composer</term> and <term ref="#academic">academic</term>.</p>
      </div>
      <div type="music" n="2">
        <head>Genres</head>
        <p>His music is associated with <term ref="#synthPop">synth-pop</term>, <term ref="#hypnagogicPop">hypnagogic pop</term>, <term ref="#postPunk">post-punk</term>, <term ref="#experimentalPop">experimental pop</term> and <term ref="#loFi">lo-fi</term>. These genres are encoded as concepts because they describe both musical style and cultural classification.</p>
      </div>
      <div type="education" n="3">
        <head>Education</head>
        <p><persName ref="#johnMaus">John Maus</persName> studied at <orgName ref="#calArts">California Institute of the Arts</orgName>, <orgName ref="#egs">European Graduate School</orgName> and <orgName ref="#universityHawaii">University of Hawaiʻi</orgName>. At <orgName ref="#calArts">California Institute of the Arts</orgName>, he met <persName ref="#arielPink">Ariel Pink</persName>.</p>
      </div>
      <div type="network" n="4">
        <head>Related People</head>
        <p>The project records <persName ref="#arielPink">Ariel Pink</persName> as a collaborator, <persName ref="#pandaBear">Panda Bear</persName> as a performance connection, and <persName ref="#alainBadiou">Alain Badiou</persName> as a philosophical reference.</p>
      </div>
      <div type="network" n="5">
        <head>Organizations</head>
        <p><persName ref="#johnMaus">John Maus</persName> is connected to <orgName ref="#hauntedGraffiti">Ariel Pink's Haunted Graffiti</orgName>, <orgName ref="#ribbonMusic">Ribbon Music</orgName>, <orgName ref="#upsetTheRhythm">Upset the Rhythm</orgName> and <orgName ref="#young">Young</orgName>.</p>
      </div>
      <div type="discography" n="6">
        <head>Albums</head>
        <p>Important albums include <title ref="#songs">Songs</title>, <title ref="#loveIsReal">Love Is Real</title>, <title ref="#pitilessCensors">We Must Become the Pitiless Censors of Ourselves</title>, <title ref="#rarities">A Collection of Rarities and Previously Unreleased Material</title>, <title ref="#screenMemories">Screen Memories</title>, <title ref="#addendum">Addendum</title> and <title ref="#laterThanYouThink">Later Than You Think</title>.</p>
      </div>
      <div type="intertextuality" n="7">
        <head>Textual Phenomenon</head>
        <p>The album title <quote source="#fifteenTheses">We Must Become the Pitiless Censors of Ourselves</quote> is encoded as a quotation and citation because it refers to <title ref="#fifteenTheses">Fifteen Theses on Contemporary Art</title> by <persName ref="#alainBadiou">Alain Badiou</persName>.</p>
      </div>
    </body>
  </text>
  <standOff>
    <listPerson>
      <person xml:id="johnMaus"><persName>John Maus</persName><birth when="1980-02-23"><placeName ref="#austinMinnesota">Austin, Minnesota</placeName></birth><idno type="wikidata">https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q5980315</idno><idno type="wikipedia">https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/John_Maus</idno></person>
      <person xml:id="arielPink"><persName>Ariel Pink</persName><idno type="wikidata">https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q47839</idno><idno type="wikipedia">https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ariel_Pink</idno></person>
      <person xml:id="pandaBear"><persName>Panda Bear</persName><idno type="wikidata">https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q1386857</idno><idno type="wikipedia">https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Panda_Bear_(musician)</idno></person>
      <person xml:id="alainBadiou"><persName>Alain Badiou</persName><idno type="wikidata">https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q29153</idno><idno type="wikipedia">https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alain_Badiou</idno></person>
    </listPerson>
    <listOrg>
      <org xml:id="calArts"><orgName>California Institute of the Arts</orgName><idno type="wikidata">https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q1026924</idno></org>
      <org xml:id="egs"><orgName>European Graduate School</orgName><idno type="wikidata">https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q1374137</idno></org>
      <org xml:id="universityHawaii"><orgName>University of Hawaiʻi</orgName><idno type="wikidata">https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q687697</idno></org>
      <org xml:id="hauntedGraffiti"><orgName>Ariel Pink's Haunted Graffiti</orgName></org>
      <org xml:id="ribbonMusic"><orgName>Ribbon Music</orgName><idno type="wikidata">https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q7321144</idno></org>
      <org xml:id="upsetTheRhythm"><orgName>Upset the Rhythm</orgName></org>
      <org xml:id="young"><orgName>Young</orgName></org>
    </listOrg>
    <listPlace>
      <place xml:id="austinMinnesota"><placeName>Austin, Minnesota</placeName><idno type="wikidata">https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q486822</idno></place>
      <place xml:id="losAngeles"><placeName>Los Angeles</placeName><idno type="wikidata">https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q65</idno></place>

XSLT transformation to HTML

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<xsl:stylesheet version="1.0" xmlns:xsl="http://www.w3.org/1999/XSL/Transform" xmlns:tei="http://www.tei-c.org/ns/1.0">
  <xsl:output method="html" encoding="UTF-8" indent="yes"/>
  <xsl:template match="/">
    <html lang="en"><head><meta charset="UTF-8"/><title>John Maus TEI Output</title>
      <style>body{font-family:Arial,sans-serif;background:#f4f4f4;color:#222;padding:40px}main{max-width:1100px;margin:auto;background:white;padding:40px;border-radius:16px;box-shadow:0 8px 28px rgba(0,0,0,.08)}h1,h2,h3{color:#173f5f}p{font-size:18px;line-height:1.75}.tag{background:#eef6ff;border-bottom:2px solid #173f5f;padding:1px 4px;border-radius:4px}table{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;margin:18px 0 32px;font-size:14px}th{background:#173f5f;color:white}th,td{border:1px solid #ccc;padding:10px;text-align:left;vertical-align:top}</style>
    </head><body><main>
      <h1><xsl:value-of select="tei:TEI/tei:teiHeader/tei:fileDesc/tei:titleStmt/tei:title"/></h1>
      <p><strong>Encoded by: </strong><xsl:value-of select="tei:TEI/tei:teiHeader/tei:fileDesc/tei:titleStmt/tei:author"/></p>
      <h2>Encoded Text</h2>
      <xsl:for-each select="tei:TEI/tei:text/tei:body/tei:div">
        <h3><xsl:value-of select="tei:head"/></h3>
        <p><xsl:apply-templates select="tei:p/node()"/></p>
      </xsl:for-each>
      <h2>People</h2><table><tr><th>ID</th><th>Name</th><th>Authority</th></tr><xsl:for-each select="tei:TEI/tei:standOff/tei:listPerson/tei:person"><tr><td><xsl:value-of select="@xml:id"/></td><td><xsl:value-of select="tei:persName"/></td><td><xsl:for-each select="tei:idno"><xsl:value-of select="@type"/>: <xsl:value-of select="."/><br/></xsl:for-each></td></tr></xsl:for-each></table>
      <h2>Organizations</h2><table><tr><th>ID</th><th>Name</th><th>Authority</th></tr><xsl:for-each select="tei:TEI/tei:standOff/tei:listOrg/tei:org"><tr><td><xsl:value-of select="@xml:id"/></td><td><xsl:value-of select="tei:orgName"/></td><td><xsl:for-each select="tei:idno"><xsl:value-of select="@type"/>: <xsl:value-of select="."/><br/></xsl:for-each></td></tr></xsl:for-each></table>
      <h2>Places</h2><table><tr><th>ID</th><th>Name</th><th>Authority</th></tr><xsl:for-each select="tei:TEI/tei:standOff/tei:listPlace/tei:place"><tr><td><xsl:value-of select="@xml:id"/></td><td><xsl:value-of select="tei:placeName"/></td><td><xsl:for-each select="tei:idno"><xsl:value-of select="@type"/>: <xsl:value-of select="."/><br/></xsl:for-each></td></tr></xsl:for-each></table>
      <h2>Albums and Textual References</h2><table><tr><th>ID</th><th>Title</th><th>Author</th><th>Date</th></tr><xsl:for-each select="tei:TEI/tei:standOff/tei:listBibl/tei:bibl"><tr><td><xsl:value-of select="@xml:id"/></td><td><xsl:value-of select="tei:title"/></td><td><xsl:value-of select="tei:author"/></td><td><xsl:value-of select="tei:date"/></td></tr></xsl:for-each></table>
      <h2>Relations</h2><table><tr><th>ID</th><th>Relation</th><th>Active</th><th>Passive</th></tr><xsl:for-each select="tei:TEI/tei:standOff/tei:listRelation/tei:relation"><tr><td><xsl:value-of select="@xml:id"/></td><td><xsl:value-of select="@name"/></td><td><xsl:value-of select="@active"/></td><td><xsl:value-of select="@passive"/></td></tr></xsl:for-each></table>
    </main></body></html>
  </xsl:template>
  <xsl:template match="tei:persName|tei:placeName|tei:orgName|tei:term|tei:title|tei:quote"><span class="tag"><xsl:apply-templates/></span></xsl:template>
</xsl:stylesheet>

RDF output.

The RDF/Turtle dataset turns the TEI entities and relations into triples. John Maus becomes a FOAF person; albums become music albums; genres become SKOS concepts; and authority-control links are expressed with owl:sameAs.

RDF/Turtle sample

@prefix ex: <http://example.org/john-maus/> .
@prefix foaf: <http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/> .
@prefix schema: <https://schema.org/> .
@prefix dcterms: <http://purl.org/dc/terms/> .
@prefix skos: <http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#> .
@prefix owl: <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#> .

ex:johnMaus a foaf:Person ;
  foaf:name "John Maus" ;
  schema:birthPlace ex:austinMinnesota ;
  schema:alumniOf ex:calArts, ex:egs, ex:universityHawaii ;
  schema:memberOf ex:hauntedGraffiti ;
  schema:performer ex:pandaBear ;
  dcterms:creator ex:songs, ex:loveIsReal, ex:pitilessCensors, ex:rarities, ex:screenMemories, ex:addendum, ex:laterThanYouThink ;
  schema:genre ex:synthPop, ex:hypnagogicPop, ex:postPunk ;
  owl:sameAs <https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q5980315> .

ex:arielPink a foaf:Person ; foaf:name "Ariel Pink" ; owl:sameAs <https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q47839> .
ex:pandaBear a foaf:Person ; foaf:name "Panda Bear" ; owl:sameAs <https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q1386857> .
ex:alainBadiou a foaf:Person ; foaf:name "Alain Badiou" ; owl:sameAs <https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q29153> .

ex:austinMinnesota a schema:Place ; schema:name "Austin, Minnesota" ; owl:sameAs <https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q486822> .

ex:calArts a foaf:Organization ; foaf:name "California Institute of the Arts" ; owl:sameAs <https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q1026924> .
ex:egs a foaf:Organization ; foaf:name "European Graduate School" ; owl:sameAs <https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q1374137> .
ex:universityHawaii a foaf:Organization ; foaf:name "University of Hawaiʻi" ; owl:sameAs <https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q687697> .
ex:hauntedGraffiti a foaf:Organization ; foaf:name "Ariel Pink's Haunted Graffiti" .
ex:ribbonMusic a foaf:Organization ; foaf:name "Ribbon Music" ; owl:sameAs <https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q7321144> .

ex:songs a schema:MusicAlbum ; schema:name "Songs" ; schema:datePublished "2006" .
ex:loveIsReal a schema:MusicAlbum ; schema:name "Love Is Real" ; schema:datePublished "2007" .
ex:pitilessCensors a schema:MusicAlbum ; schema:name "We Must Become the Pitiless Censors of Ourselves" ; schema:datePublished "2011" ; dcterms:publisher ex:ribbonMusic ; dcterms:source ex:fifteenTheses ; owl:sameAs <https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q7977682> .
ex:rarities a schema:MusicAlbum ; schema:name "A Collection of Rarities and Previously Unreleased Material" ; schema:datePublished "2012" .
ex:screenMemories a schema:MusicAlbum ; schema:name "Screen Memories" ; schema:datePublished "2017" .
ex:addendum a schema:MusicAlbum ; schema:name "Addendum" ; schema:datePublished "2018" .
ex:laterThanYouThink a schema:MusicAlbum ; schema:name "Later Than You Think" ; schema:datePublished "2025" .

ex:fifteenTheses a dcterms:BibliographicResource ; dcterms:title "Fifteen Theses on Contemporary Art" ; dcterms:creator ex:alainBadiou .

ex:synthPop a skos:Concept ; skos:prefLabel "synth-pop" ; owl:sameAs <https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q753110> .
ex:hypnagogicPop a skos:Concept ; skos:prefLabel "hypnagogic pop" .
ex:postPunk a skos:Concept ; skos:prefLabel "post-punk" .
ex:experimentalPop a skos:Concept ; skos:prefLabel "experimental pop" .
ex:loFi a skos:Concept ; skos:prefLabel "lo-fi" .
ex:politicalPhilosophy a skos:Concept ; skos:prefLabel "political philosophy" .

From biography to linked data.

The project transforms John Maus from an encyclopedic subject into structured knowledge through a Digital Humanities workflow. Starting from selected information derived from Wikipedia and Wikidata, the project develops a theoretical model, a conceptual ontology, a TEI/XML encoding, and an RDF representation of the domain.

By combining semantic modelling, text encoding, and Linked Open Data principles, the project demonstrates how a cultural subject can be transformed into structured, interoperable, and reusable knowledge.

Digital Humanities and Digital Knowledge
University of Bologna

John Maus · The Music Messenger · LODLAM Project
